entry.title: Principal propagation setup as SAP Java backend and SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Principal Propagation Setup as Java Backend

SAP BTP =>Cloud Connector =>SAP NetWeaver AS Java

  1. Introduction

This blog explains how to configure principal propagation using X.509 certificate for an SAP NetWeaver AS Java backend (HTTPS). SAP Cloud Connector generates a client certificate for the user and forward it to the backend system.

  1. Architecture Overview

User =>BTP App/Work Zone =>BTP Destination (PrincipalPropagation) => SCC => AS Java Backend.

Key requirements:

  • Mutual TLS between SCC and backend for HTTPS propagation (backend must trust SCC system cert/CA chain).
  • CA Certificate configured in SCC can issue user certificate.
  • User mapping rules on AS Java (ClientCertLoginModule) to map certificate fields to UME users.
  1. Prerequisites Checklist

3.1 Backend user attribute alignment (SU01)

SAP Backend user email/user ID must match with BTP identity.

Ensure the email in the backend user record matches the email used to log in to BTP.

3.2 Cloud Connector certificates

You need three certificate

  1. System Certificate (for trust between SCC and backend)
  2. CA Certificate (to issue client certificate)
  3. User Sample Certificate (rule based mappting).

NOTE : SAP Supports Self-signed certificate for ABAP based systems, but Java not supports Self-signed and the SCC system certificate/Backend server certificate must be signed by CA.

Java Systems: Java-based systems (like SAP Portal or PI/PO) use a KeyStore-based trust model. While they can technically support self-signed certificates, But SAP's implementation for Principal Propagation via the Cloud Connector requires a Certificate Authority (CA) chain for validation.

3.3 Permissions & access

-SCC admin access

-BTP subaccount admin for Destinations

-AS Java admin access to NetWeaver Administrator (NWA) security and SSL configuration.

  1. Step-by-Step Configuration

Step 1 - Maintain backend user mapping attribute (Email)

In backend user maintenance (ABAP SU01 / Java UME as applicable), fill E-Mail Address exactly as used in BTP.

Pick one mapping strategy (Email OR UserID) and use it consistently across BTP <-> IdP <->backend.

Here we taken Email strategy.

Step 2 - Generate/Import SCC certificates (System + CA + Sample User Cert)

2.1 System Certificate (SCC)

In Cloud Connector Admin UI:

  1. Go to Configuration -> On Premise -> System Certificate
  2. Create CSR and get it signed by CA (recommended).

  1. Import the certificate received from your AD team.

 

Also import the signed system certificate in AS Java trust store (NWA=>Cert & keys=>key storage=>Trusted CA).

NOTE: Import chain certificate (binded=>actual certificate and RootCA).

2.2 CA Certificate (SCC)

  1. Go to Configuration -> On Premise -> CA Certificate
  2. CA Certificate can be self-signed or the same way you can generate CSR & import the signed CA certificate.

 

2.3 User Sample Certificate

Maintain subject pattern as email and generate the sample certificate.

 

Create sample certificate.

 

-Import the created sample certificate in NWA=>Cert & keys=>key storage=>TicketKetstore

Step 3 - Sync Cloud Trust with IdP in SCC

Sync the trust configuration for principal propagation.

  1. Go to Cloud To On-Premise -> Principal Propagation
  2. Click Sync to fetch IdP/trust configuration from the subaccount context.

 

Without this trust, SCC can’t validate the inbound identity and won’t be able to generate the user certificate.

Step 4 - Configure cloud to On-prem mapping Your backend system mapping must be configured for HTTPS and identity forwarding.

  1. Go to Cloud To On-Premise → Access Control
  2. Create or edit your mapping for the backend system (HTTPS)
  3. Set Principal Type => X.509 Certificate
  4. Maintain resource paths (for example / or /sap/ depending on your requirement).

 

Set Principal Type = X.509 Certificate for the certificate-based identity.

 

Step 5 - Configure BTP Destination Authentication = PrincipalPropagation

In BTP:

  1. Navigate to Connectivity -> Destinations
  2. Create/update the Runtime destination to the backend via SCC
  3. Set:

Proxy Type: OnPremise

Type : HTTP

Authentication: PrincipalPropagation

URL points to virtual host/port from SCC mapping

  1. Backend Setup (SAP NetWeaver AS Java)

Step 6 - Activate HTTPS port in AS Java

AS Java must accept HTTPS and be able to request/require client certificates.

Configure SSL:

  1. Open: http://<host>:<port>/nwa     =>ssl
  2. Add a new access point with protocol HTTPS (ignore if HTTPS already active)

  1. Ensure SSL library is available and the SSL status becomes OK
  1. Secure/Apply SSL certificate for Java backend.

Your Java HTTPS endpoint certificate must be valid for the hostname used by SCC.

  1. Create/generate CSR for CA signing and import your CA response and root CA

     

Note : While generate the CSR don’t, forgot to maintain SAN (Subject Alternate Name) address, without SAN URL cannot be secured.

Tip: SAN can be FQDN and hostname of Java backend.(if you have two java instance generate CN = *.domain.com.

You can refer the SAP Note to generate the CSR & Certificate import using sapgenpse cmd line.

       2275562 - How to import sapgenpse generated PSE file into AS Java

Step 7 - Define Rule-Based Mapping (ClientCertLoginModule)

Converting the certificate identity into a UME user.

-Add ClientCertLoginModule

-Define rules for mapping certificate fields (Subject CN / SubjectAlternativeName/email) to a UME user.

NWA=>Authentication and Single Sign-On =>Authentication

Maintain below authentication stacks for Policy client_cert.

 

- Configure Clientcertloginmodule (Create Mapping rules)

-Enable Certificate Logon

-PrincipalMappingLoginModule

Step 8 – Define Reverse Proxy parameters

Add the parameter
icm/trusted_reverse_proxy_<X> = <SCC System Certificate Subject, Issuer>

This reverse proxy parameter used to establish the trust relationship between the SAP Backend and SAP Cloud connector.

NOTE : This is mandatory for Principal propagation.

Step 9 – Restart the Java services

  • Verify everything and restart the java services to make the parameters effect.

Step 10 – Validate the SSO functionality with the work zone application URL.

SUMMARY:
After completing the successful principal propagation setup as java backend, below are the recommendations.

  1. For Java based systems use the CA signed certificate.
  2. Ensure SAN entries are added in the SSL certificate.
  3. Generate sample certificate in SCC and import it in Ticketkeystore of NWA.
  4. Maintain only required fields => AttributeName, getUserFrom and UserMappingMode for ClientCertLoginModule.

 Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone: Tile Opens(content federation) but App Fails with ‘IP Address Cannot Be Found
entry.content:
In my SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition) environment, I faced an issue where a dynamic tile loaded successfully through content federation, but clicking the tile caused the application to fail with a browser error:

ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED – The IP address of the server could not be found

The failing URL always contained a very long auto‑generated hostname.

 

Interestingly, the dynamic tile itself worked correctly (e.g., OData count displayed), but navigating to the corresponding SAP Fiori app consistently failed. This clearly indicated that the issue was not with backend connectivity or destinations, but with the content runtime URL generated by Work Zone.
 
Please refer the screenshots.

entry.title: Introducing prebuilt Joule skills in SAP Build digital workspaces: Get more done with simple prompts
entry.content:
If your day involves spinning up new spaces, hunting for the right workspace, or sharing content with your team, you know the clicks can add up. SAP Build simplifies this in its digital workspace experience with new prebuilt Joule skills which turns plain‑language prompts into actions. Ask Joule to create or find a workspace and it does the work in the background- then gives you a direct link in the chat so you can jump right in

The first set of prebuilt Joule skills are already live. Take a look at what’s available today: 

 

What’s in this first set of skills

  • Create workspaces from a prompt
    • Example: “Create a workspace called ‘Q3 Onboarding Hub’ using the standard onboarding template.”
    • Joule creates and names the workspace behind the scenes and returns a link in the chat.
  • Find workspaces you need
    • Example: “Show me all workspaces I’m a member of related to ‘Onboarding’.”
    • Joule lists relevant workspaces right in the chat, helping you get where you need to go faster.
  • Add documents right from chat
    • Drag and drop a file into the Joule chat, confirm the target workspace, and Joule adds it for you- no tab hopping required.
  • Find and add members to workspaces
    • Tell Joule who you want to add to a workspace, and Joule finds the person, confirms and sends them an invitation to join the workspace.

Why this matters

For teams needing a space to collaborate and produce results, these skills simplify their day-to-day so that -

  • members spend less time on common setup tasks like launching a project hub, onboarding center, or campaign space,
  • users can move from “search and navigate” to “ask and act” without leaving their flow of work,
  • workspace creators get consistency in content through standardized workspace templates to get started quickly, 
  • business users get more self-service options and admins get fewer “how do I create/find X?” tickets.

Prebuilt skills can be used in conjunction with the existing AI capabilities already available in digital workspaces. For example, once you find or create workspaces via Joule, you can use embedded AI assistance to create and publish content or summarize documents within the workspace.

Further, Joule can be enabled with document grounding for workspaces so that it answers questions based on content maintained in workspaces and links back to sources. This way its responses are grounded and relevant to business context.

Admins and developers can also build custom skills for Joule using Joule Studio and use them in digital workspaces.

Everyday scenarios where you can use these skills:

  • HR onboarding hub: “Create a workspace called ‘New Hire Hub – July’ based on the onboarding template.” Share the link with HR/ team managers in minutes.
  • Project kickoff: “Find all workspaces mentioning ‘Apollo Launch’.” Jump straight into the right workspace for plans, assets, and status.
  • Policy refresh: “Create a workspace named ‘FY26 Policy Updates’.” Drag and drop updated PDFs into the chat to populate the workspace content folder quickly.
  • Campaign workspace: “Create ‘Q2 Partner Enablement’.” Then use AI writing assistance on the landing page to publish a clean, on‑brand overview.
  • Knowledge triage: “Show my ‘Customer Escalations’ workspaces.” Get a list in chat and dive into the relevant one to update content or add the right members to solve the issue.

Getting started with Joule’s prebuilt skills and embedded AI in digital workspaces:

    • First, the admin must ensure Work Zone is enabled as a Joule launcher as per the instructions in this guide
    • Once set up is done, open Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone and try prompts like
      • “Create a workspace named ‘Team All‑Hands’.”
      • “Find my workspaces related to ‘Compliance’.”
    • Ask your admin to enable document grounding for the workspaces you rely on- so Joule can answer questions with citations.
    • Ask your admin to enable embedded AI features to use AI assistance when editing pages or posts to speed up content creation once your workspace is ready or to summarize your documents quickly. See how you can use embedded AI for document summarization or workspace content creation here: 

 

Tips for Best Results for interacting with Joule:

  • Be specific with search keywords while searching for workspaces or content with Joule
  • While prompting give sufficient context to help Joule understand the task.

What’s next
This is just the first set of prebuilt skills we’re releasing for digital workspaces. We’re continuing to expand the catalog with more actions and smarter multi-step flows.

entry.title: Configure SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Logistics Management Content Package
entry.content:
Configure SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Logistics Management Content Package

Introduction

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, serves as a centralized digital workplace that aggregates business applications, processes, and information into a unified user experience. For SAP Logistics Management users, integrating the Content Package with SAP Build Work Zone enables streamlined access to logistics operations, real-time insights through integration cards, and personalized user preferences—all within a single, intuitive interface.

This guide walks you through configuring SAP Build Work Zone with the SAP Logistics Management Content Package, enabling features such as predefined integration cards, user preference plugins, and enhanced collaboration capabilities. The target audience includes SAP BTP administrators, logistics managers, and IT professionals responsible for system integration and user experience optimization.

Prerequisites

Before beginning the configuration, ensure the following requirements are met:

  • SAP Build Work Zone Subscription: Active subscription to SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition under the same SAP BTP subaccount as SAP Logistics Management
  • SAP Logistics Management Access: Valid subscription and access to SAP Logistics Management tenant
  • SAP BTP Cockpit Access: Administrator-level access to SAP BTP Cockpit for destination and role management
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services: Access to the Administration Console for configuring application dependencies
  • User Roles: Appropriate role collections assigned for content management and site administration
  • Content Package Files: Download the latest Content Package files from SAP Note 3605828, including:
    • LGMIntegrationCards_0.0.101.zip (Integration Cards)
    • UserPreferencePlugin (User Preferences Plugin)
    • LGMJoulePlugin_1.0.0.mtar (Joule Plugin)

What is SAP Build Work Zone?

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition is a cloud-based digital workplace solution that provides a central entry point to SAP and non-SAP applications. It enables organizations to create personalized, role-based business sites that aggregate content from multiple sources, improving productivity and user engagement ,.

Key Capabilities:

  • Unified launchpad for accessing SAP applications
  • Integration of UI Integration Cards for real-time insights
  • Role-based content delivery and personalization
  • Spaces and pages for flexible content organization
  • Support for SAP Companion for in-app help

Benefits for SAP Logistics Management Users:

  • Centralized access to logistics applications and data
  • Real-time visibility into warehouse operations, shipments, and inventory
  • Personalized dashboards with relevant KPIs and alerts
  • Seamless navigation between logistics processes
  • Enhanced collaboration through integrated communication tools

Integration Architecture: The integration follows a content exposure and consumption model where SAP Logistics Management acts as a content provider, exposing apps, roles, and integration cards to SAP Build Work Zone through standardized APIs and content packages ,.

What is the SAP Logistics Management Content Package?

The SAP Logistics Management Content Package is a pre-configured bundle of business content designed specifically for logistics operations. It includes integration cards, user preference plugins, and application tiles that provide immediate value upon deployment.

Key Components:

  • Integration Cards: Predefined cards displaying essential logistics updates such as warehouse status, shipment tracking, and inventory alerts
  • User Preference Plugin: Allows users to set default preferences like default warehouse, enhancing personalization
  • Joule Plugin: Enables AI-powered assistance within SAP Build Work Zone for logistics tasks,
  • Roles and Permissions: Pre-configured role collections for logistics users
  • Spaces and Pages: Organized content structure for logistics workflows

Business Value:

  • Accelerated time-to-value with ready-to-use logistics content
  • Consistent user experience across logistics processes
  • Reduced configuration effort through pre-built components
  • Enhanced decision-making with real-time logistics insights

Configuration Steps

Step 1: Configure Integration Between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Logistics Management

1.1 Define Application Dependency in SAP Cloud Identity Services

Navigate to the Administration Console for SAP Cloud Identity Services and configure the integration between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Logistics Management:

  • Access Bundled Applications for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  • Create a new dependency with the following properties:

Property

Value

Dependency Name

`` (e.g., LGM_Backend_Access)

Application

SAP Logistics Management ()()

API

backend-access

This dependency enables SAP Build Work Zone to consume SAP Logistics Management APIs for integration cards.

Step 2: Create Destinations in SAP BTP Cockpit

2.1 Create Integration Cards Destination

In the SAP BTP Cockpit, create a destination named LGM_Integration_Cards with the following configuration:

Property

Value

Name

LGM_Integration_Cards

Type

HTTP

URL

.lgm.cloud.sap/api (refer to Local Launchpad documentation)

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

NoAuthentication

Additional Properties

 

HTML5.DynamicDestination

true

HTML5.ForwardAuthToken

true

HTML5.IASDependencyName

`` (from Step 1.1)

This destination enables secure communication between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Logistics Management for integration card data.

Step 3: Deploy the Content Package

3.1 Download Content Package Files

Download the latest Content Package files from SAP Note 3605828,

  • LGMIntegrationCards_0.0.101.zip
  • UserPreferencePlugin MTAR file
  • LGMJoulePlugin_1.0.0. mtar

3.2 Deploy User Preference Plugin

Using Cloud Foundry CLI, deploy the User Preference Plugin:

Copy code -->cf deploy

Example:

Copy code --> cf deploy mta_archives/UserPreferencePlugin_0.0.2.mtar

Step 4: Upload Content Package to SAP Build Work Zone

4.1 Access Channel Manager

  1. Access SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  2. Navigate to Channel Manager
  3. Select HTML5 Apps
  4. Click New Content Package

4.2 Upload Content Package

  1. Browse to the LGMIntegrationCards_0.0.101.zip file
  2. Set a Runtime Destination (use LGM_Integration_Cards created in Step 2)
  3. Click Upload
  4. Verify the upload status by clicking the Report link.

The report should confirm that the Content Package contains roles, apps, spaces, and pages.

Step 5: Update Content in SAP Build Work Zone

5.1 Refresh HTML5 Apps

  1. In Channel Manager, select HTML5 Apps
  2. Click Update Content to complete the deployment to SAP BTP

5.2 Add Content to Content Manager

  1. Navigate to Content Manager → Content Explorer → HTML5 Apps
  2. Locate User Preferences in the HTML5 Apps table
  3. Click Add to include it in your site content.

Step 6: Assign Roles and Permissions

6.1 Configure Role Assignments

  1. In Content Manager, search for the role (e.g., ContentPackageRole),
  2. Verify that apps and spaces are assigned to the role
  3. Click Edit on the role detail page
  4. Enable Assignment Status for User Preferences
  5. Click Save

6.2 Assign Role Collection to Users

  1. Navigate to SAP BTP Cockpit → Security → Users
  2. Select the target user
  3. Click Assign Role Collection
  4. Search for and assign ContentPackageRole

Step 7: Create and Configure the Business Site

7.1 Create a New Site

  1. Open SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  2. Navigate to Site Directory
  3. Click Create Site and provide a meaningful name

7.2 Configure Site Settings

  1. Go to Site Settings
  2. Click Edit
  3. In the Assignments area, assign ContentPackageRole to the site by clicking '+'
  4. Ensure Spaces and Pages - New Experience is selected
  5. Click Save

The new site experience provides a flexible grid-based layout with spaces and pages based on the Horizon visual theme.

Step 8: Test the Integration

8.1 Access the Business Site

  1. Click the Go to site icon to open the business site
  2. Verify that the SAP Logistics Management integration cards are visible on the home page

8.2 Validation Checklist

  • Integration cards display real-time logistics data
  • User preferences are accessible and functional
  • Navigation to SAP Logistics Management apps works correctly
  • Role-based content is properly filtered
  • Joule plugin (if deployed) is operational

Key Features Available After Configuration

Feature 1: Predefined Integration Cards

SAP Logistics Management provides several predefined integration cards that display essential logistics updates directly on the SAP Build Work Zone home page. These cards offer real-time visibility into:

  • Warehouse operations and capacity
  • Shipment status and tracking
  • Inventory levels and alerts
  • Order fulfillment metrics

Feature 2: User Preference Plugin

The User Preference Plugin allows users to set default preferences such as default warehouse, streamlining their daily workflows. Users can:

  • Configure personal logistics settings
  • Save frequently used parameters
  • Customize dashboard views
  • Set notification preferences

Feature 3: Joule AI Assistant

The Joule Plugin enables AI-powered assistance within SAP Build Work Zone for logistics tasks. Users can:

  • Ask natural language questions about logistics data
  • Receive intelligent recommendations
  • Automate routine logistics tasks
  • Access contextual help and guidance

Troubleshooting Common Issues

Issue 1: Content Package Not Visible

Possible Causes:

  • Incorrect destination configuration
  • Missing role assignments
  • Content not refreshed in Channel Manager

Resolution Steps:

  1. Verify the LGM_Integration_Cards destination is correctly configured with all required properties
  2. Ensure the dependency in SAP Cloud Identity Services is properly set up
  3. In Channel Manager, click Update Content to refresh HTML5 Apps
  4. Check that the role collection is assigned to both the site and the user

Issue 2: Authentication Errors

Possible Causes:

  • Incorrect IASDependencyName in destination
  • Missing ForwardAuthToken property
  • Trust configuration issues

Resolution Steps:

  1. Verify HTML5.IASDependencyName matches the dependency name created in SAP Cloud Identity Services
  2. Ensure HTML5.ForwardAuthToken is set to true
  3. Check trust configuration between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Logistics Management
  4. Validate user authentication in SAP Cloud Identity Services

Issue 3: Integration Cards Not Loading Data

Possible Causes:

  • Incorrect destination URL
  • Network connectivity issues
  • Missing backend permissions

Resolution Steps:

  1. Verify the destination URL format:  ..lgm.cloud.sap/api
  2. Test connectivity from SAP BTP to SAP Logistics Management
  3. Ensure the user has appropriate authorizations in SAP Logistics Management
  4. Check SAP Logistics Management system logs for API errors

Best Practices

Regular Content Package Updates

  • Monitor SAP Note 3605828 for new Content Package versions
  • Test updates in a non-production environment before deploying to production
  • Communicate changes to end users before updates

User Training Recommendations

  • Provide role-specific training on integration card usage
  • Create user guides for setting preferences
  • Conduct workshops on leveraging Joule AI assistants

Performance Optimization

  • Limit the number of integration cards per page to improve load times
  • Use caching strategies for frequently accessed data
  • Monitor system resource consumption

Security Considerations

  • Regularly review and update role assignments
  • Implement least privilege access principles
  • Enable audit logging for sensitive logistics data
  • Maintain clickjacking protection allowlists

Additional Resources

  • SAP Help Portal: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition | SAP Help Portal | SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Community: A Guide to Developing and Deploying Content Packag... - SAP Community
  • SAP Note 3605828: 3605828 - Configure the Build Work Zone with the Content Package of SAP Logistics Management - SAP for Me
  • GitHub Samples: build-workzone-integration/standard at main · SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration · GitHub
  • SAP Learning Journeys: SAP Work Zone | SAP Learning

Conclusion

Configuring SAP Build Work Zone with the SAP Logistics Management Content Package transforms the user experience by providing centralized access to logistics operations, real-time insights, and personalized workflows. By following this guide, you have successful:

  • Established secure integration between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Logistics Management
  • Deployed predefined integration cards for logistics visibility
  • Enabled user preference customization for enhanced productivity
  • Configured role-based access to logistics content

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone: Tile Opens(content federation) but App is opening blank in Mobile
entry.content:
I am currently integrating a custom role and tile from an on‑premise S/4HANA system into SAP Build Work Zone using the CDM-based content federation (Exposure Version 2).

Everything works fine in Build Work Zone in the browser.
However, when I scan the QR code in SAP Start mobile app, the tile appears correctly — but after tapping the tile, the app opens a blank white screen.

 

 

Has anyone experienced this issue where a tile loads correctly in Build Work Zone, but shows a blank screen when launched from SAP Start?

entry.title: Old Tile Still Appearing in SAP Build Work Zone After Complete Re‑Exposure from S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am facing an issue with federated content from an on‑premise S/4HANA system to SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition).
Our authorization team has removed the old tile and assigned a new tile to the correct custom role, custom catalog, and custom group in S/4HANA.

To ensure a clean setup, I deleted the previous exposure configuration and re‑created the full setup from scratch in:

  • /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE (content exposure)
  • SAP BTP → Build Work Zone → Content Provider → Synchronize Content

Despite repeating the full process multiple times, the old tile continues to appear in Build Work Zone instead of the new one.
The authorization consultant has verified both the new role and the tile assignments several times on the S/4HANA side, and everything looks correct there — but Build Work Zone keeps federating only the old tile.

Has anyone experienced something similar?

I am trying to understand:

  • Why outdated tile information is still being federated
  • Whether additional cleanup is required on the CDM or content provider side
  • If there are any cache layers (S/4HANA, BTP, or Build Work Zone) that must be explicitly cleared
  • Whether Exposure Version 2 requires specific steps to refresh or replace previously published app IDs

Any advice, known issues, or guidance on how to force Build Work Zone to pick up the updated tile would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule health checks
entry.content:
As a UX specialist in the SAP S/4HANA Regional Implementation Group, my team and I often get to work with customers keen to leverage the significant capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule.

With so much interest in Joule and so many use cases available, our aim is to make it easier for you to configure your use cases efficiently and support your business users as they start to use Joule every day.

Good news! In this blog we cover simple checks to ensure that your SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, and Joule architecture is correctly configured and working properly before you onboard your business users.

Performing these checks will allow you to become more confident and leverage the significant benefits of Joule.

NOTE 1: There are 2 different build plans for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. Further details on the foundation and standard plans can be found here: SAP Note: 3704047 - Comparison of Joule functionalities while using SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition using foundation plan or standard plan. 

NOTE 2: The simple tests detailed here are intended to be run AFTER SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule have been fully configured.

NOTE 3: We are planning on releasing 3 , excellent, short videos on Joule support and debugging on https://learning.sap.com/ in the near future. Check back here for details!

 

Architecture

Below, you can see the relationship of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, Joule, SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), SAP Cloud Identity Service, (CIS), SAP Identity Authentication Services (IAS) and SAP Identity Provisioning Services (IPS) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

Understanding this architecture is critical to testing and troubleshooting your landscape!

NOTE: The above architecture diagram does not include the SAP Web Dispatcher as additional component between the SAP Cloud Connector and SAP S/4HANA. If you use an SAP Web Dispatcher then this needs to be included in any troubleshooting.

Prerequisites

Make sure that you have met all prerequisites before you expose content to your launchpad. It is essential that SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule have been configured correctly as per the following:

  • SAP Help: Joule Onboarding Guide

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, standard edition - integration with Joule

  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

  • SAP Note: 3523238 - Navigational and Transactional capabilities with Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

1.    Introduction

You can test if Joule has been technically correctly configured by prompting Joule to fetch information from the backend system, e.g. ‘Display Sales Order 911’ as in the example below:

Sample Joule capability

Based on the reply from Joule, you can proceed as follows:

- If Joule provides answers with specific sales orders -> the technical configuration is fine

- If Joule does not provide answers, this could have multiple causes:

  • Principal Propagation is not working

  • SAP Identity Provisioning service jobs have not run correctly or have run with errors

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition or content exposure is not correct

  • Timeouts in the backend

  • Role assignment is not correct

  • Other errors

In the following sections we will go through the steps associated with checking the above in more detail.

 

2. Principal Propagation

Principal Propagation is the authentication mechanism that SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, Joule use to authenticate users, and it is essential that it is configured properly. You can check if your Principal Propagation configuration is correct as follows:

  • Test from Joule using a transactional capability as mentioned in the Introduction section of this blog

  • In case you have or can easily add an SAP Business Application Studio instance to the Joule subaccount, test the  "S4HANA_PCE_SSO" destination with Principal Propagation configured using curl command as described in this blog : How to check the connectivity to your backend system in Business Application Studio

  • Or, from a browser, you can simulate a Principal Propagation request also with the “foundation” plan of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition (NOTE: This test is more technical!):

           Prerequisite: Check your test user has the necessary role to read Common Data Model (CDM) content (role SAP_FLP_EXP_USER enables viewing of exposed content)

           Refer to: SAP Fiori Launchpad: Manage Launchpad Content Exposure

           The steps for the Principal Propagation simulation test are as follows:

            1. Create a copy of the S4HANA_PCE_SSO destination and call it ‘authtest’

            2. In this new destination ‘authtest’, Add parameter “HTML5.DynamicDestination = true”

            3. An example of the new ‘authtest’ destination is shown below:

Copied destination for Principal Propagation test

                 NOTE: After Principal Propagation has been fully validated, please DELETE the ‘authtest’ destination

            4. The CDM URL is either one of the following, depending on what version of CDM you are using:

                 V1: /sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/entities

                 V2: /sap/bc/http/ui2/flp_content_exposure/entities

                 Version 2 Content Exposure is preferred if this option is available on your system.

                 (NOTE: Make a note of the V1 or V2 URL for use in subsequent steps)

            5. Check the CDM service directly on the SAP S/4HANA system, open the local FLP and test the CDM URL:

Test the CDM URL

 

              6. Open the SAP Build Work Zone instance & copy the entire URL right up to the first ‘/’ and paste in Notepad:

              7. Remove the ‘dt.’ from the URL in the previous step

              8. Add ‘/dynamic_dest/authtest’ to the end of the URL. Note: ‘authtest’ is the copied destination we created in step 1

              9. Construct the URL to test CDM service passing through SAP Build Work Zone and the SAP Cloud Connector:

 

                   Successful Principal Propagation test

 

           10. Successful execution of this URL proves Principal Propagation is working correctly!

Principal Propagation troubleshooting tools are as follows:

       (a) SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition transactions:

                - SMICM (activate HTTP trace level 1)

                - CERTRULE (check rule-based mappings)

                Refer to SAP Note: 2351619 - How to take SMICM trace?

         (b) SAP Cloud Connector logs

Refer to How to troubleshoot Cloud Connector principal propagation over HTTPS

NOTE: We are currently updating our Principal Propagation enablement content, check back here for details!

 

3. SAP Cloud Connector

When you are testing Principal Propagation (in #2), you could consider to temporarily turn on SAP Cloud Connector logs (at a suitably appropriate time of low system usage) which will detect errors such as:

  • Principal Propagation and authentication issues

  • Backend server connectivity issues (e.g., incorrect hostname or protocol, expired certificates)

  • Service issues (e.g., inactive or not found)

In the example below, you can see that authentication has failed due to an expired certificate.

SAP Cloud Connector - expired certificate

 If an error does not show up as expected, you can consider increasing the trace level of the logs as shown below. For SSL issues, a TLS trace has to be active, and for other issues, you need to look at the tunnel trace.

SAP Cloud Connector - trace levels

You should also turn on SAP Cloud Connector Alerting so you can be kept informed about critical system issues (expiring certificates, disk space, CPU load etc…) BEFORE THEY OCCUR.

THIS IS ESPECIALLY IMPORTANT IN YOUR PRODUCTION LANDSCAPE!!!!!

EXAMPLE: By turning on Alerts for expiring certificates, an email alert would be sent 30 days before a certificate expires, so a new certificate can be generated and used, thereby preventing any connectivity issues:

Refer to SAP BTP Connectivity - Alerting

 

4. Check your content exposure

You have already run transaction code /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE to expose your chosen roles to SAP BTP. Refer to:

  • SAP Help: Manage Launchpad Content for Exposure

  • Blog: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

Now, you can check if your content has been successfully exposed and is ready for consumption by SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).

Exposure is available in two versions: the current Version 1 and the new Version 2 as follows:

  • Version 1 is based on legacy target mappings

  • Version 2 provides a seamless end-to-end app model, extending from the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library to business user usage. Version 2 is based on Launchpad App Descriptor Items (LADIs) and is the long-term, future-proof successor to Version 1.

NOTE: Since apps have different IDs in version 2, there is no compatible upgrade path from version 1 to version 2. Implementing integration using version 2 requires initiating a new project.

Depending on whether you are using Version 1 or Version 2, the following URLs can be used to test your content exposure to SAP BTP:

  • Version 1 http://<your-server-name>:<your-port-number>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/entities

  • Version 2 http://<your-server-name>:<your-port-number>/sap/bc/http/ui2/flp_content_exposure/entities

Version 1 and 2 have different URLs, 1 is based on classical tiles and target mappings and 2 is based on LADIs.

The URLs must be run in the browser (not via SAP BTP) and expose content in JSON format. Below, you can see that SAP Fiori App F0233A using semantic object ‘OutboundDelivery’ and action ‘displayFactSheet’ has successfully been exposed from a backend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system and can  be read by SAP BTP.

Test the url directly in a  browser (not via SAP BTP) with the system user configured in the design time destination (JOULE_ADMIN in the configuration guide).

Testing content exposureRefer to:

  • SAP Note: 3501486 - Launchpad Content Exposed with version 2 not updated in SAP Build Workzone, Standard edition

  • SAP Note: 3345119 - Expose Launchpad Content to SAP BTP - Exposure Version 2

  • SAP Help: Manage Launchpad Content for Exposure

 

5. SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Now you can check your Content Channel, and the associated destinations work correctly

  • Check that the Design Time and Runtime destinations (‘jouledt’ and ‘joulert’) work successfully:Destination TestsExample: An incorrect Design Time destination will cause errors importing roles from SAP S/4HANA Cloud  Private Edition. NOTE: The Ping does *not* fully check the entire destination. If it is “red” you know something is wrong, but “green” does not mean that everything is fine

  • You can check if your Content Channel synchronization works successfully (NOTE: the synchronization will not work if  the destination pings in (a) have not been successfully executed). Click on the ‘Synch’ button to consume content exposed from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition:

  • Click on the ‘Report’ link to check that there are no synch errors:

  •  Check the Content Explorer to ensure that content exposed through a channel has been successfully consumed by SAP BTP- From the Site Manager page, select Content Manager and then Content Explorer:

  • Chose the Channel through which your content was consumed by SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition:

  • Here you can see the content consumed through your selected channel. In this example, you can see that 31 roles have been exposed from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and consumed by SAP BTP.   

  • Check roles exposed from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition have been successfully consumed by SAP BTP:

  • Check that your test user has the correctly exposed roles assigned:

        NOTE: If you do not see any roles here, check the following:

  • The test user has the necessary roles in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  • Roles have been exposed via Content Exposure

  • SAP Identity Provisioning service jobs have run and completed successfully

Refer to:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, standard edition - troubleshooting

  • Integration Guide: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, Standard Edition Integration with SAP S/4HANA

 

6. Identity Provisioning service

The navigation service of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition uses the SAP Identity Provisioning service (IPS). IPS provisions identities and their authorizations between source and target systems. To use the navigational capabilities in Joule, you need to configure SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition as the source and SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, standard edition as the target system in IPS. Here, you confirm successful provisioning by reviewing logs and status of synchronizing jobs.

- In SAP Cloud Identity Services, view Identity Provisioning -> Provisioning Logs:

Provisioning logs

- Now you can see the Job Execution Logs and check them to ensure there are no errors:

Execution logs

 - In this example, you can see that user BPINST cannot be provisioned correctly by SAP IPS as it has NO email address -> this  user will experience issues with SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule.

Example execution log

7.    Joule Welcome

Once all the above tests have been successfully executed, you can run Joule. If Joule has been configured correctly then there will be a welcome screen with Joule skills showing as in the example below:

Joule debugger console

Click on the debug icon shown above to check there are no errors in the debugger console:

Now you can go ahead and start using Joule and leverage its business value!

 

8. Help! I have tried all the above checks and still have problems

Don’t worry! There are lots of great troubleshooting tools.

a)  Check if the related Fiori apps on the SAP S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad are working without issues:

      App Support: Troubleshoot configuration and authorization errors for apps in the SAP Fiori launchpad.

     Refer to:

  • App Support
  • Setting Up App Support

       App Inspector: Troubleshoot configuration and authorization errors for apps available through the SAP Fiori Launchpad

  Refer to:

  • App Inspector
  • Setting Up App Inspector

b) In Joule, you have the Debug button (click on the ‘Debug’ button show below):

  • Highlights service issues and general errors
  • Active conversation with Joule
  • Response Payload: Shows aggregated JSON response for each Joule interaction
  • Requests Logs: Shows requests and responses grouped by prompt

Joule debug console

 c)  SAP Cloud Connector Logs. These logs can have the following use cases:

  • Principal propagation and authentication troubleshooting
  • Backend server connectivity (e.g., incorrect hostname or protocol, expired certificates)
  • Service issues (e.g., inactive or not found)

 The logs can be accessed in the SAP Cloud Connector as shown:

SAP Cloud Connector logs

Refer to SAP Help: Cloud Connector Trace and Logging

d) SAP Gateway Error Log

  • Transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG
  • Shows service errors (e.g., forbidden, not found, inconsistent calls)

      SAP Gateway Tracing Tools

  • Transaction /IWFND/TRACES
  • Troubleshoot service issues, capture header and payload data

      Internet Communication Manager Monitor

  • Transaction SMICM
  • Detailed HTTP calls (by increasing trace level to level 3)
  • Helps to identify authentication and SSL issues

       Refer to SAP Help: Gateway Tracing Tools

Still stuck? Refer to SAP Help: Joule - Monitoring and Troubleshooting which includes details on raising SAP incidents.

Troubleshooting:

  • Blog: Common configuration issues: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Help: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, standard edition - Troubleshooting
  • SAP Help: How to troubleshoot Cloud Connector principal propagation over HTTPS
  • SAP Help: Joule Constraints
  • SAP Note: 3666014 - Information required for Joule troubleshooting 
  • SAP Note: 3649670 - Missing Joule icon in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition Standard Edition
  • SAP Note: 3666014 - Information required for Joule
  • SAP Note: 3673511 - Troubleshooting for Joule page display issues
  • SAP Note: 3679363 - How to recognize a Joule response's

Becoming an SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on the community topic page for SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Other helpful links in the SAP Community:

  • Follow our tag S4HANA RIG - SAP Community for more from the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG
  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: SAP Joule – Common Configuration Issues
entry.content:
Are you interested in activating Joule and integrating it seamlessly into your system—but running into errors along the way? In my role as solution architect for our SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and Regional Implementation Group I’ve worked with some of our first Joule customers and would like to share how you can avoid and resolve errors during the activation.

This blog highlights the most common SAP Joule issues encountered during provisioning, booster execution, and system integration.

You’ll also find clear, step-by-step guidance to help you quickly troubleshoot and succeed with your activation.
Please also refer to our SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule Health Check blog to test your activation and configuration is working correctly before using Joule https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition-and-joule-health-checks/ba-p/14348321

We will look at the following topics:

  1. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) subaccount / provisioning issues
  2. Joule booster problems
  3. User Experience (UX) / icon appearance issues
  4. SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning service (IPS) and Identity Authentication service (IAS) issues
  5. Restrictions/ constraints
  6. Monitoring and troubleshooting
  7. References
  8. How to contact SAP Support

1. SAP BTP subaccount / provisioning issues

Issue

Solution/Available fix

You have a license for an SAP product that supports Joule integration (such as SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, etc.) and after creating a subaccount the Joule service is not available in the subaccount entitlement.

Check SAP Help:Data Centers Supported by Joule

The data centre is supported but the entitlements are not available in your global account.

Contact your SAP Account Manager/Executive. 

Refer to SAP Note 3639572 - Enabling Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

SAP Help:Joule prerequisites

The menu entry "System Landscape" is not visible in the Global account of your SAP BTP Cockpit.

You are not an administrator of the Global account.

SAP Note 3456718 - Menu "System Landscape" is not visible in the Global account of SAP BTP Cockpit

SAP Note 2669325 - How to add a user as a SAP Business Technology Platform Global Account Administrator

SAP systems are not visible in SAP BTP under the system landscape.

 

 

Before adding your system, make sure it's not already auto-discovered and listed automatically on the System Landscape ---> Systems page.

Refer to SAP Help: BTP Registering SAP System
SAP Note 3661817 - SAP systems are missing in System Landscape of the BTP Cockpit

 2. Joule Activation/Issues during the execution of the Joule booster

While running the booster configuration for the Joule Service, the prerequisites check failed for the Identity Authentication Tenant.

 An error message is displayed: "Your global account does not have an Identity Authentication service tenant."

 

Ensure that the IAS tenant is visible under the global account and configure trust between the Identity Authentication Service (IAS) and the Joule subaccount. 

SAP Note 3675105 - Joule Setup Error: "Your global account does not have an identity authentication service tenant"

 

 

 

The Joule booster is being run, and you encounter the following error:

*********************************
Could not find SAP Cloud Identity system with host <IAS tenant>. 
Report an incident in the BC-CP-MP component 

The SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant isn't connected to the correct SAP BTP Global Account, or it's linked to the wrong customer ID.
How to Fix it:
a. Make sure your IAS tenant is linked to the correct Global Account.
Check the SAP Help:View Assigned Tenants and Admins.

b. If you have multiple IAS tenants , use the IAS tenant that matches the Global Account's customer ID.
Important:Data syncing between systems usually takes up to one week.Wait for this to complete before running the booster again so that the SAP IAS system appears in all the accounts.

You can manually trigger the sync via the “Refresh” button.
SAP Note 3620409 - Could not find SAP Cloud Identity system with host: Joule booster error

Enabling Joule leads to the following error: 
"Prerequisites for the formation type "Integration with Joule" are not fulfilled.
No SAP Cloud identity service system is showing up under“System landscape” --->System

 

A new SAP BTP Global Account will take some time to synchronize all existing customer systems, but you can manually trigger the sync via the "Refresh" button.
This should ensure that your SAP Cloud Identity Services system shows up. Afterwards you can proceed with the Joule configuration.

 

The SAP Build Work Zone system does not appear in the dropdown list during the setup process (Joule Booster - Step 5 Set Up Integrations).

 

 

The SAP Build Work Zone service was not subscribed to under the relevant subaccount.
An instance of this service is not enough for the integration; therefore, the system will not appear under the options.

Add the SAP Build Work Zone subscription in the Joule subaccount. Refer to
SAP Note 3710864 - SAP Build Work Zone system not visible in dropdown during setup

Unable to find the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition option in Step 3 - "Select Integration" stage dropdown during activation by means of the Joule Booster.

In global account Systems, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system is already auto-discovered.

Ensure that it is visible in Joule Booster Step 04  "Select Capabilities", select "S/4HANA Private Cloud" and proceed to complete the Joule booster.

Refer to SAP Note 3710921 - S/4HANA Private Cloud option is not listed in Step 3 - "Select Integration" in Joule Booster for detailed information.

Error message: "The subscription of subaccount <xxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxx> to application das-application and plan foundation, failed with state: SUBSCRIBE_FAILED."

Observe the error messages indicating SaaS Subscription failed and instructions to open a support case or unsubscribe and retry.

 

Refer to SAP Note 3689452 - Entity Saas Subscription failed after running Joule Booster for detailed instructions.

If the issue persists, raise a support incident and choose component CA-JOULE-PRV-ONB for further investigation.

 

 

 3. UX/ Icon appearance issue

Joule refused to frame 'https://xxx.xxx.cloud.sap/' because an ancestor violates the following Content Security Policy directive: "frame-ancestors”.                             

 

 

 

 

Enable 3rd party cookies of your browser.Add the trusted domain in SAP IAS and SAP BTP
a. Add trusted domain in SAP IAS in the SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant.
Go to Applications & Resources -->Tenant Settings --> Trusted Domains--> Click the + (Add) button
Type the URL of the trusted host into the field.

b. Add the trusted domain in SAP BTP.
In SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to your specific subaccount ---> Security ---> Settings.
Click the + (Add) button.
Enter the required hostname, for example: https://<your-s4-or-sfsf-url>.com

The Joule icon at the top app bar in the Fiori Launchpad is not properly displayed.

The issue is caused using an unsupported SAPUI5 version in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system. 

 

Upgrade the SAPUI5 Component version of SAP S/4HANA to be equal to or higher than the minimum supported version.

SAP Notes:
2346367 - How to upgrade the SAPUI5 library in ABAP - SAP for Me
3641945 - Joule icon displayed wrongly in Fiori launchpad of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
 

After integrating Joule with SAP Solutions, the user is unable to launch Joule by clicking the diamond icon,  it fails to load or respond.

 

 

Allow third-party cookies.

SAP Note 3643889 - User unable to launch the Joule Icon after integrating with SAP Solutions

The Joule icon is not appearing for a specific user despite that the correct role-based permissions have been assigned in SAP SuccessFactors.

The issue is most likely caused by security policies of the network provider, which may be blocking certain URLs that are required for the icon to appear.
The affected user does not have the General User Permission --->Access to Joule

Refer to SAP Note 3685621 - Joule button not appearing on SuccessFactors

After clicking on the Joule icon in SAP SuccessFactors, it tries to load for a few minutes and subsequently the user gets a blank or greyed-out screen.

Refer to SAP Note 3544482 - Getting Error / White /Blank / IAS login Screen When Trying to Launch Joule in SuccessFactors

4. SAP IPS/IAS issues

The SAP IAS source system read job is failing.

 

Keep the source system name the same as in the content channel that had been created in the standard SAP Build Work Zone.

Joule was successfully integrated with the SAP product. However, clicking the Joule icon results in a blank white or SAP IAS login screen. Single sign-on is not working.

 

Check the SAP IAS domain. In the SAP BTP Joule subaccount, go to Trust Configuration and select the Custom Identity Provider for Applications.

Change the field "Domain" to the one used in the SAP product.

Refer to SAP Note 3635949 - Blank white / IAS login screen appears when clicking on Joule icon from the solutions where Joule was integrated into

5. Restrictions /Constraints
SAP Joule operates within specific constraints.
SAP notes:
3698807 - How to Remove or Hide the Joule Icon in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
3711505 - Restrictions: Multiple different Work Zone editions integration with Joule
Before starting the Joule project, please refer to the document below. All constraints applicable to Joule are listed here.
SAP Help:Joule constraints

6. Monitoring and troubleshooting
You may experience issues that would have to be reported in SAP incidents. In such cases, please collect information, which will help the support team to investigate and resolve the problem quickly.
SAP Note 3666014 - Information required for Jouletroubleshooting
SAP Help:Monitoring and Troubleshooting

7. How to report issues
You can report an incident or error through SAP for Me. Ensure that you provide a detailed description of the issue including the steps to reproduce it. Attach any relevant screenshots, logs, or error messages to help with the investigation. Please use the appropriate components listed below when creating your incident.

General support component for core Joule services issue and functionality

CA_JOULE

Joule integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

CA-FLP-EXT-PCE-JOULE

SaaS Subscription error after running Joule Booster

CA-JOULE-PRV-ONB

8. Frequently asked questions

  1. Are there options available to customize the theme of Joule so it aligns with our branding guidelines and specific colour palette?

The theme tab is only available when Joule is used in a standalone (single page) mode. For now, Joule automatically adapts to the theme of the embedding application.  For example, if the user will changes the theme in the SAP Work Zone settings, Joule will automatically adopt it. The user cannot choose a different theme for the Joule client.
SAP Note 3646989 - Is it possible to adjust theming for Joule?

  1. Is it possible to remove the Joule icon for selected users?

When Joule is enabled through the Joule booster, it is activated for all users in both SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud systems. At present, there is no supported option to restrict or disable Joule for specific users or user groups.
SAP Note 3698807 - How to Remove or Hide the Joule Icon in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Summary
In this blog, we reviewed common configuration and activation issues related to Joule including challenges with SAP Business Technology Platform provisioning, Joule booster activation, UX visibility, and integration with SAP Cloud Identity Services (IPS/IAS), along with key restrictions, monitoring tips, and reference resources.

In addition, we discussed current system restrictions and limitations, monitoring options, log analysis techniques, and practical troubleshooting steps to help identify and resolve issues efficiently.

By following the guidance and best practices outlined above, administrators can streamline Joule activation, minimize configuration errors, and ensure a smoother user experience. If problems persist, contact SAP Support with detailed information such as error messages, screenshots, tenant details, and relevant logs to accelerate root cause analysis and resolution.

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team

entry.title: How to Integrate ABAP Derived Roles in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Overview

If you are integrating your Fiori apps from SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP Build Work Zone, you may face the challenge of exposing derived and composite roles containing launchpad content.

Creating (PFCG) roles on ABAP system, that derive from roles with launchpad content, is mainly driven by the need to differentiate authorization for the same set of applications. This often leads to many roles deriving from the same base role. In such cases, the derived roles are assigned to users instead of their base roles. The derived roles get the content of the role menu, like Fiori catalogs or spaces from inheritance and focus mainly on authorizations and user assignments.

This blog outlines the approach for exposing derived roles from an ABAP system to SAP Build Work Zone.

Related Blog

In the blog How to Integrate ABAP Composite Roles in SAP Build Work Zone you will learn two options when exposing composite roles. The recommended approach for integrating derived roles is the same as outlined in “Option 2: Indirect Exposure via Single Roles (with IPS)” when exposing composite roles.

Integrating Derived Roles in SAP Build Work Zone

When exposing derived roles to SAP Build Work Zone there is a separation of concerns between the exposure of launchpad content, authorization and role-to-user assignment.

For the exposure, you select only the single roles that contain launchpad content in /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE. Derived roles are not supported in the exposure report. This restriction prevents exposing a high number of roles with identical launchpad content. When the content for exposure is processed, the system identifies roles which derive from the selected roles and includes these references in the exposed CDM content (as relatedRoleAssignments). These references are not exposed to the administrator during exposure but used by the Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) and SAP Build Work Zone infrastructure.

 

Identity Provisioning Service and SAP Build Work Zone handle the role assignment logic, ensuring that users with derived roles on the ABAP side get the correct roles assigned on the BTP side. With that, users get access to the application they are authorized for.

Key points

  • Each exposed single role includes information about roles deriving from it and their relationships, but those derived roles are not directly available in the Content Manager on BTP.
  • The IPS ensures that when an user is assigned to a derived role in ABAP, all required single roles (now exposed) are assigned in Work Zone/BTP. For further information, see  Configure Integration with the Identity Provisioning Service and Exploring Role Assignments
  • This approach is supported since SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release 2022 and later.

 

Conclusion 

Integrating derived roles with SAP Build Work Zone works best with IPS-driven provisioning, as it preserves existing role structure and automated user to role assignment. Without IPS one must manually ensure that corresponding roles in both BTP and ABAP system are assigned, which is impractical in larger scenarios. The IPS based approach also supports the integration of composite roles, which are often used together with derived roles.  In that case only single roles should be exposed. Information about related derived or composite roles is provided though CDM content and processed by SAP Build Work Zone and IPS.

 

Further Information

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition - Restrictions

entry.title: SAP Mobile Start Troubleshooting Guide
entry.content:
Ensuring a smooth mobile experience is essential for users relying on SAP Mobile Start to access business applications, notifications, and content on the go. But often already during the initial setup that administrators perform – setting up and integrating content into SAP Mobile Start & SAP Build Work Zone – various issues can appear before the app ever reaches end users.

When something isn’t working in SAP Mobile Start, it’s easy to assume the app is the problem. But in reality, most issues come from the surrounding configuration - such as the general service and Identity Provider (IdP) setup, content integration, or how web applications behave inside the mobile in app browser.

In this post, I’ll share my recommendations on how to narrow down where an issue is possibly coming from and highlight the problems & their causes we see most often. Below, I’ll walk you through practical checks you can use to pinpoint and possibly resolve the root cause yourself. And in case a ticket is required, the guidance here should help you provide the right information upfront, speeding up analysis and resolution significantly.

Layers of SAP Mobile Start & How to Detect Where an Issue Comes From 🔎

When troubleshooting SAP Mobile Start, it helps to understand that issues typically fall into one of two areas: the native layer of the app or the web layer of the in-app browser. Knowing which layer is affected gives a good indication of how to continue finding root cause.

1. Native Layer  📱

The native layer covers everything that comes directly from the SAP Mobile Start app itself. This includes:

  • The initial onboarding flow
  • Device registration and connection to SAP Build Work Zone & SAP Mobile Services
  • Native app features such as widgets, push notifications, Spotlight search integration, and app settings
  • Rendering the in-app screens, such as content on the “Start” tab, application tiles, cards and their menu structure on the “Apps” tab or Tasks / Situations on the “To-Do” tab.

If something goes wrong before tapping a tile, there’s a good chance the issue lies in the native layer. Typical examples include a failing onboarding process, errors showing the dynamic values on DynamicAppLauncher tiles, missing tiles or content, or issues related to notifications.

2. Web Layer  🌐

Once a user taps a tile which points to a web application, SAP Mobile Start transitions to the web layer. This means an in-app browser is used inside SAP Mobile Start to open content such as:

  • SAPUI5 / Fiori apps
  • Custom web applications or URLs
  • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition Workspaces & Pages
  • Any external web-based tool integrated as an application tile

Because these apps run inside an embedded in-app browser, misconfigurations originating from the “web world” often surface here. These may include security related issues (authentication, session handling, 3rd party cookies), the mobile readiness of the integrated applications, or specific environment/integration configurations. Typical symptoms include repeated login screens or apps opening to a blank page.

In-app browser options:
Another topic worth mentioning in the context of the web layer is the in‑app browser options available in SAP Mobile Start. Depending on the platform, two browser types are supported:

  • iOS: Optimized SAP Mobile Start In-App Browser based on the WKWebView (default) and SFSafariViewController (in-app variant of the standard Safari browser)
  • Android: Chrome Custom Tabs (default – provided by the default browser app installed on the device) and WebView

Which browser is used is controlled through feature flags in the SAP Mobile Services Admin Cockpit for SAP Mobile Start.

SAP Mobile Services - Feature Flags

 This is also where several security features are offered (e.g., clipboard protection, blocking of downloads, etc.) to provide built in Mobile Application Management (MAM) capabilities for the embedded browser environment. These security features rely on the WebView variant, meaning that once any of them is activated, WebView becomes the active browser on both iOS and Android.

On iOS specifically, there is an additional browser feature flag that explicitly sets the WebView based variant, which has an optimized UX for launching Fiori apps in SAP Mobile Start, as the default option. This flag must be disabled if you want to switch to SafariViewController.

I mention this because there are scenarios where switching the browser can be useful - for example, when working with certificate-based authentication, certain Conditional Access policies, or when analyzing web apps that fail to load in the default browser. Testing the same app using the alternative browser option can help isolate whether the issue is browser specific.

For further information on the browser options please refer to our help page: Browser Experience in SAP Mobile Start.


To summarize this section a quick rule of thumb. The issue happens…

  • …before a tile opens the in-app browser? → Native layer.
  • …after a tile opens the in-app browser? → Web layer.

 

General Troubleshooting Steps  🛠

When considering steps for troubleshooting, the above classification of the issue into the native or web layer should be considered. Let’s start with steps to analyze and collect information on issues within the native layer.

Troubleshooting the SAP Mobile Start Native Layer

  1. Re-do the onboarding
    Make sure to log out of the app completely and onboard again. Additionally uninstalling/reinstalling could make sense to assure a clean starting point. Make sure you are using the correct registration QR Code, Deep Link or in case of MDM onboarding, the right parameters. Especially with Deep-Link or MDM-based onboarding, wrong values in these configurations may lead to a failed onboarding flow.

  2. Reproduce the issue on another device / OS
    If possible, test it on a different device on the same platform & on a second device on the other OS compared to where the issue was initially found (iOS vs. Android).
    In similar lines test if the issue affects only specific users or is present for everyone.

  3. Check role and content assignments
    Ensure that the user has the necessary roles assigned to see the content. Also make sure that content is assigned properly to the SAP Build Work Zone site:
    • Roles need to be assigned to the Site & Users
    • Apps need to be assigned to Roles and Groups or Pages
    • Pages need to be assigned to Spaces
    • Spaces need to be assigned to Roles

     Additionally, if content is not appearing in SAP Mobile Start, it might be caused by the fact that the content is not flagged to support the current device form-factor (e.g. phone) or is of an unsupported content type.

  4. Collect logs when required
    SAP Mobile Start supports two levels of logs for further analysis:
    1. Client Logs, which cover deep app functionality like onboarding, native behaviors etc.
    2. Network Traces, that cover connectivity via SAP Mobile Services for e.g. API calls in the native context.

    Both can be very helpful to further narrow down the root cause. Especially when creating a ticket, it’s very helpful for the team to check them and see if any abnormalities surface.

    The upload of Client Logs needs to be enabled inside the Mobile Services Admin Cockpit. Then they can be uploaded via the app’s user menu. Find more information on our Troubleshooting and Support page.

    For the Network Trace the recording needs to be started by an admin inside the Mobile Services Admin Cockpit, you can follow the official documentation. The process is also described under point 2.4 of SAP Note 3582668 - Troubleshooting for SAP Mobile Start.

Analyze the Web Layer Issues in SAP Mobile Start

Once a tile is opened, SAP Mobile Start transitions to the web layer. At this point, content runs inside the in‑app browser (WebView, SafariViewController, or Chrome Custom Tabs depending on platform and feature flags). Authentication flows and SAP Build Work Zone or application configuration come into play.

As mentioned earlier these kinds of issues are what we see most of the time (~80+%) and don’t necessarily directly relate to SAP Mobile Start. The following steps help to further identify whether the issue is originating from outside of the Application: 

  1. Test whether the issue also occurs outside SAP Mobile Start
    This might sound trivial, but many issues we see are reproducible inside a standard or mobile browser. However, they might not show initially as browser behaviors, cookie settings and active sessions keep them hidden.
    1. Open the same content in SAP Build Work Zone on Desktop

      Use a fresh incognito/private window and ensure that 3rd‑party cookies are blocked (this mirrors the default behavior of most mobile browsers – as especially the iOS operating system is very restrictive with 3rd party cookies and cross-domain scenarios). Open the site and the specific application you’re having issues with, and check whether everything behaves as expected. Typical indicators to watch for:

      • Basic Authentication popups ➡️ may indicate failing Principal Propagation
      • Second login screen or error screen when opening a tile  ➡️ often caused by incorrect IdP configuration or differing domains between SAP Build Work Zone and the integrated application
      • Blank Screen ➡️ can be indication that SAP UI5 Version of the backend system is out of maintenance (see also Removing outdated UI5 versions from UI5 CDN - SAP Community and 3001696 - Removed outdated SAPUI5 versions from SAPUI5 CDN - Fiori applications might stop functioning - SAP for Me)

       

    2. Open SAP Build Work Zone in a standalone mobile browser

      If step a. worked without issues, continue by testing the same content in a mobile browser (Safari on iOS, Chrome on Android). Mobile browsers behave differently from desktop browsers and more closely reflect the environment SAP Mobile Start uses internally.
      This helps to verify whether the issue is related to mobile browser behavior, restrictions, or mobile‑specific rendering.

    3. Open the direct application URL

      For the final and most “realistic” test, copy or share the exact application URL from the embedded browser inside SAP Mobile Start. Note that in case you changed security settings the sharing function might be disabled via feature flag.

      This URL will directly open the Application – without the manual navigation action from the SAP Build Work Zone Site - and can reveal differences in authentication or rendering behavior that may not show up during steps a or b. We frequently see incorrect configuration causing that e.g. principal propagation is not correctly working with the SAP UI5 app runtime of the target system when called directly. Such issues are not always appearing when using the SAP Build Work Zone Site on Desktop Browsers, when for example OData requests of dynamic tiles have by chance created a backend session but that is not the case when launching such apps from SAP Mobile Start due to the separate layers.


      If the issue can be reproduced in one of these checks, it is very likely that the root cause lies in the general configuration (e.g., authentication) or in the application itself rather than in SAP Mobile Start app and the configuration itself should be further investigated. If not, the next step may provide additional clues.


  2. Compare behavior between in-app browsers

    Switching between:

    • WebView <> SafariViewController (iOS)
    • ChromeCustomTabs <> WebView (Android)

    and comparing the behavior can be a helpful next step to identify differences in how the in‑app browsers handle your application. In general, the system‑browser variants (SafariViewController on iOS and Chrome Custom Tabs on Android) behave more similarly to standalone mobile browsers, while the WebView may differ slightly or lack certain capabilities. You can switch the browser options as outlined in the “Web Layer” section above. Note that switching the browser will force all users to redo their onboarding! So rather test this before an official rollout or within a non-productive environment.


If none of these checks provide further insights and the issue cannot be reproduced anywhere except inside the in‑app browser of SAP Mobile Start, the final step might be to reach out to the team via the standard ticketing process.

  

Last resort – Creating a Ticket for SAP Mobile Start  📨

After running through the checks above, you should have a much clearer picture of where the issue originates. Maybe you could even figure it out by checking on the general configuration, role assignments, or web content integration.

If you’re still stuck after all this, it might be time to create a ticket — and please, make it a good one! With the information you gathered during these steps, you’re in an excellent position to provide the details we need to analyze the issue quickly and accurately. Here’s what helps us analyze issues faster and more precisely:

  1. A meaningful title
    Avoid generic titles like “SAP Mobile Start not working.”
    Choose something that reflects the actual issue (e.g., “Blank screen when launching Fiori app from Mobile Start on iOS”). This helps support teams quickly differentiate between cases.

  2. The correct support component
    Use the outcome of your earlier checks:
    • If the issue also occurs in SAP Build Work Zone via a browser, it’s likely a SAP Build Work Zone or application configuration topic → consider raising a ticket under the SAP Build Work Work Zone component, find more information on their Getting Support page.

    • If the issue only occurs in SAP Mobile Start, use:
      Component: MOB-APP-SMS

  3. The right priority
    Choose a priority that matches the actual business impact.
    SAP provides clear rules and guidance (see SAP Note on incident priorities).

  4. Technical information
    Include the basics:
    • Device OS (iOS / Android / both) and OS version
    • Installed SAP Mobile Start version (incl. build number)

  5. Detailed error description & reproducible steps
    Be as concrete as possible:
    • Exact steps to reproduce the issue
    • Does it occur always or intermittently?
    • Is it affecting all users/devices or only specific ones?

  6. Helpful attachments
    Visual material is extremely valuable. Attach Screen recordings (best case) or Screenshots. 
    These help us immediately understand what you’re seeing.

  7. A test user
    If possible, provide a dedicated test user.
    This allows the support to reproduce the issue directly and significantly accelerates the analysis.

  8. Steps you already performed
    Summarize what you’ve tested using the guide above. For example:
    • If you suspect the native layer:
      Attach SAP Mobile Start Client Logs + Traces captured while reproducing the issue.
    • If it occurs in the web layer:
      Let us know whether the app works in standalone browsers (desktop, mobile, incognito).

Providing this information upfront not only speeds up the entire support process but also makes it much easier for us to understand where the issue is coming from. The more complete your initial ticket is, the fewer back‑and‑forth clarification steps are needed — meaning you’ll get to a solution faster and with less effort.

Conclusion

I hope this guide helps you get a clearer picture of how to approach troubleshooting in SAP Mobile Start and gives you a solid toolkit for narrowing down issues more quickly. With a bit of structured testing and the right checks, many problems can be identified — or even fully resolved — long before a ticket is needed.

And for those cases where a ticket is the right next step: I’m looking forward to seeing even better, more comprehensive ticket descriptions in the future! 😉 It really does make a huge difference in how fast we can support you.

Thanks for reading, and feel free to share feedback or questions in the comments!


____________________________

For further information on the new topics, please check our SAP Mobile Start documentation.

SAP Mobile Experience offers intelligent native mobile solutions that help businesses build more efficient, resilient and sustainable end-to-end processes, improving people’s work life wherever they are.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the S/4HANA Public Edition (Pt D: Improvements)
entry.content:
Introduction

Now Artificial Intelligence (AI) is on every professional’s mind. Everybody wants to be on the bandwagon.  I plan to write a series of blogs on this topic under a big umbrella “My AI Experience Sharing”.  This is the first mini-series: setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

I purposely delayed my action on setting up a Joule to wait for the dust settling down during the Early Adopter phase. The intention paid off but still won’t give me a free pass 😉. There are five credible references available as listed in the Reference section, but none of them give you a good explanation on Why, especially on What in a critical step with a readable picture when you desperately need a hint.  To help all the people like me, this blog will focus on explaining those steps, what to enter, and possible mistakes to avoid. 

This week, I re-did the Joule Setup on a Parallel Line of the SAP S/4HANA ERP Public Edition, aka Public Cloud. I noticed a few changes and would like to outline here for your reference:

You can install Joule on your Parallel Line

Several weeks ago, I met one of our customers at the Customer Success Day in NYC. After listening to his rational behind requiring Joule on the Parallel Line, I promised to do a test. I did just that this week on a Parallel Line. It worked.

No need to NavigationService

I heard there is an improvement in the Setup process, i.e., no more need to manually create the NavigationService destination (outlined in Step B.2.4 of my blog My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the S/4HANA Public Edition (Pt B:Joule Installation). If you continue to add this destination, it won’t hurt because the system is no longer seeking the information stored there. I will remove this step in my blog.

Joule Booster is Faster

The Joule Booster is noticeably faster.  In my case, it is about 15 minutes to complete the running.

SAP Work Zone Content Provider
